caption:
4️⃣ MUST VISIT SECRET BARS OSAKA 

Here are FOUR of my favourite hidden bars in Osaka

Bible Club -  a speakeasy hidden behind a bible club facade 
Bar Call Me Tell Me - bar hidden behind a telephone booth! 
Bar Nayuta - hidden inside a cloak room
My FAVORITE out of the Four! - its inside a SUBMARINE! keep watching to find out where!

Osaka bound? Comment “OSAKA” and I’ll send you my Osaka cheat sheet complete with a Japan pre travel check list and everything you need to see, eat, do and drink in Osaka Japan!

𝙁𝙤𝙡𝙡𝙤𝙬 @phatlifeproject for more PHAT things to try, see, eat and do in cities around the world

Hidden bars in Osaka Japan, Secret speakeasy Osaka, Best hidden bars in Osaka, Unique themed bars Osaka, Secret cocktail bars

#osaka #osakajapan  #osakatrip #secretbar #hiddenbar speakeasy
transcript: Here are 4 secret bars you need to check out in Osaka. The first one is tucked away behind a Bible Club entrance and is seriously giving the none. Brave that though and you'll find a secret bar that makes you forget you're in Japan. Their Moscow Mew here is a must-try. Bar Call Me Tell Me is inconspicuously hidden behind a telephone booth, there's no menu here, instead tell the bartender what your preferences are and he'll make a drink customised just for you. Mine was made out of Japanese craft gin and man was it strong. Bar Nayuta was the hardest to find of them all, it's located on level 2 of an old decrepit building. Go through this discreet entrance and crouch to enter this popular hole in the wall where the crowd, deco and drinks were all 10 out of 10. Your drinks are all customised here, I enjoyed this berry gin cocktail. Though if I could only go to one next visit, it would be deepening submarine bar shinka. I mean how many people can say they've had a drink inside a submarine? Hot said, be sure to check out the bathroom while you're there.
